data.content:
Here's my analysis of all 5 project files. There are several clear improvements to make:

---

## Issues Found

### 1. No `Directory.Build.props` — massive duplication
All 5 projects copy-paste these identical properties:
```
TargetFramework, LangVersion, Nullable, ImplicitUsings,
TreatWarningsAsErrors, EnforceCodeStyleInBuild, Company, Authors
```

### 2. Package version drift (no Central Package Management)
Same packages reference **different versions** across projects:

| Package | Core | Services | Api | Api.Tests | Core.Tests |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** | **8.0.1** | **8.0.0** | — | — |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — | **17.9.0** | **17.10.0** |
| `xunit` | — | — | — | **2.7.1** | **2.8.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | — | **2.5.6** | **2.8.1** |

This version drift will cause subtle runtime inconsistencies.

### 3. Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target
Copy-pasted identically in all 3 src projects — belongs in a shared `.targets` file.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property
Set identically in all 3 src projects.

### 5. Test project boilerplate
Both test projects repeat `IsPackable=false` and the same set of test framework packages.

### 6. No `global.json`
No pinned SDK version — builds may differ across machines.

---

## Recommended Structure

Create these 4 files at the repo root:

**`global.json`** — Pin the SDK:
```json
{ "sdk": { "version": "8.0.400", "rollForward": "latestFeature" } }
```

**`Directory.Build.props`** — Shared properties + test conventions:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test project conventions -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**`Directory.Build.targets`** — Shared build targets:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

**`Directory.Packages.props`** — Central Package Management:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test packages -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` shrinks dramatically. For example, **`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** becomes:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And **`ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** becomes:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Benefits

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| 8 duplicated properties × 5 files | Defined once in `Directory.Build.props` |
| Version drift on 4 packages | Single source of truth in `Directory.Packages.props` |
| `PrintBuildInfo` target × 3 | Defined once in `Directory.Build.targets` |
| No SDK pinning | `global.json` ensures reproducible builds |
| ~130 lines of XML across projects | ~50 lines total |

Want me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **No `Directory.Build.props`** — All 5 projects repeat the same properties: `TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors`. This is the classic case for a `Directory.Build.props`.

2. **No `Directory.Build.targets`** — The `PrintBuildInfo` target is duplicated in all 3 src projects.

3. **No Central Package Management (Directory.Packages.props)** — Package versions are scattered and inconsistent:
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` ranges from `8.0.0` to `8.0.1` across projects
   - `System.Text.Json` is duplicated at `8.0.5`
   - Test SDK versions drift between `17.9.0` and `17.10.0`
   - xunit versions vary from `2.7.1` to `2.8.1`, and the runner package is also inconsistent
   - `NSubstitute` is at least consistent at `5.1.0`

There's no `global.json` to pin the SDK version, and I'm seeing repeated `DocumentationFile` settings across source projects. Test projects could consolidate their common properties and package references, and the `PrintBuildInfo` target is duplicated across all three source projects when it should live in a shared targets file.
